comment_text.sn22.49:1.3: The householder Soṇa appears here, in the next Sutta, and in SN 35.128.

root_text.sn22.49:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.sn22.49:1.3: Atha kho soṇo gahapatiputto yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami …pe…
root_text.sn22.49:1.4: ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho soṇaṁ gahapatiputtaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn22.49:2.1: “Ye hi keci, soṇa, samaṇā vā brāhmaṇā vā aniccena rūpena dukkhena vipariṇāmadhammena ‘seyyohamasmī’ti vā samanupassanti;
root_text.sn22.49:2.5: Aniccāya vedanāya dukkhāya vipariṇāmadhammāya ‘seyyohamasmī’ti vā samanupassanti;
root_text.sn22.49:2.10: aniccehi saṅkhārehi dukkhehi vipariṇāmadhammehi ‘seyyohamasmī’ti vā samanupassanti;
root_text.sn22.49:2.14: Aniccena viññāṇena dukkhena vipariṇāmadhammena ‘seyyohamasmī’ti vā samanupassanti;
root_text.sn22.49:3.1: Ye ca kho keci, soṇa, samaṇā vā brāhmaṇā vā aniccena rūpena dukkhena vipariṇāmadhammena ‘seyyohamasmī’tipi na samanupassanti;
root_text.sn22.49:3.8: aniccena viññāṇena dukkhena vipariṇāmadhammena ‘seyyohamasmī’tipi na samanupassanti;
root_text.sn22.49:4.4: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vā taṁ sukhaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn22.49:4.6: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ kallaṁ nu taṁ samanupassituṁ:
root_text.sn22.49:4.15: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vā taṁ sukhaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn22.49:4.17: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ kallaṁ nu taṁ samanupassituṁ:
root_text.sn22.49:5.1: “Tasmātiha, soṇa, yaṁ kiñci rūpaṁ atītānāgatapaccuppannaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā oḷārikaṁ vā sukhumaṁ vā hīnaṁ vā paṇītaṁ vā yaṁ dūre santike vā, sabbaṁ rūpaṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.sn22.49:6.4: yaṁ kiñci viññāṇaṁ atītānāgatapaccuppannaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā oḷārikaṁ vā sukhumaṁ vā hīnaṁ vā paṇītaṁ vā yaṁ dūre santike vā, sabbaṁ viññāṇaṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.sn22.49:7.1: Evaṁ passaṁ, soṇa, sutavā ariyasāvako rūpasmimpi nibbindati, vedanāyapi nibbindati, saññāyapi nibbindati, saṅkhāresupi nibbindati, viññāṇasmimpi nibbindati.
root_text.sn22.49:7.2: Nibbindaṁ virajjati; virāgā vimuccati. Vimuttasmiṁ vimuttamiti ñāṇaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn22.49:7.3: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānātī”ti.

translation_text.sn22.49:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.sn22.49:1.3: Then the householder Soṇa went up to the Buddha …
translation_text.sn22.49:2.1: “Soṇa, there are ascetics and brahmins who—based on form, which is impermanent, suffering, and perishable—regard themselves thus: ‘I’m better’,
translation_text.sn22.49:2.4: What is that but a failure to see truly?
translation_text.sn22.49:2.14: consciousness, which is impermanent, suffering, and perishable, they regard themselves thus: ‘I’m better’,
translation_text.sn22.49:2.17: What is that but a failure to see truly?
translation_text.sn22.49:3.1: There are ascetics and brahmins who—based on form, which is impermanent, suffering, and perishable—don’t regard themselves thus: ‘I’m better’,
translation_text.sn22.49:3.4: What is that but seeing truly?
translation_text.sn22.49:3.8: consciousness, which is impermanent, suffering, and perishable, they don’t regard themselves thus: ‘I’m better’,
translation_text.sn22.49:3.11: What is that but seeing truly?
translation_text.sn22.49:4.4: “But if it’s impermanent, is it suffering or happiness?”
translation_text.sn22.49:4.6: “But if it’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, is it fit to be regarded thus:
translation_text.sn22.49:4.7: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
translation_text.sn22.49:4.15: “But if it’s impermanent, is it suffering or happiness?”
translation_text.sn22.49:4.17: “But if it’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, is it fit to be regarded thus:
translation_text.sn22.49:4.18: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
translation_text.sn22.49:5.1: “So, Soṇa, you should truly see any kind of form at all—past, future, or present; internal or external; solid or subtle; inferior or superior; far or near: all form—with right understanding: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.sn22.49:6.1: You should truly see any kind of feeling …
translation_text.sn22.49:6.4: consciousness at all—past, future, or present; internal or external; solid or subtle; inferior or superior; far or near: all consciousness—with right understanding: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.sn22.49:7.1: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with form, feeling, perception, choices, and consciousness.
translation_text.sn22.49:7.2: Being disillusioned, desire fades away. When desire fades away they’re freed. When they’re freed, they know they’re freed.
translation_text.sn22.49:7.3: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”
